name: First Bank of Berne
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering personal, business, and agribusiness checking, savings, loans, and mortgages with free checking accounts and local community focus.
description_long: First Bank of Berne is a traditional community bank providing comprehensive financial services across personal, business, and agricultural banking segments. Based in Indiana (with a Portland, IN location mentioned), the institution operates as an FDIC-insured bank backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, serving individual consumers, small businesses, farmers, and agribusiness operations. The bank offers a broad suite of deposit and lending products including free checking accounts (personal, business, and agricultural), savings accounts, health savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, vehicle loans, secured and unsecured personal loans, credit cards, home loans, and mortgage services. They provide digital convenience through online banking with bill pay, mobile banking with mobile deposit, mobile wallet functionality, and 24/7 ATM access. For business clients, they offer cash management services, payroll processing, remote deposit capture, and positive pay fraud protection. Agricultural customers access specialized products like equipment loans, operating loans, farm financial consulting, and FSA guarantees. First Bank of Berne distinguishes itself through specialized agribusiness banking services, beginning farmer programs, and emphasis on personal relationships over automated processing. They actively support local communities, as evidenced by donations to institutions like the Jay County Public Library. The bank maintains accessibility features on their website and offers both personal and business online banking platforms with separate mobile applications. As a traditional community bank, First Bank of Berne serves customers seeking personal relationships and local decision-making rather than large national institutions. However, their service area appears geographically limited to Indiana, and they lack the technological innovation or extensive branch network of larger competitors. Customers should verify coverage in their area before banking with them.
services: Home mortgages with fixed-rate and adjustable-rate options
pros: Free checking accounts across all three segments (personal, business, agribusiness) with no monthly fees or confusing tier requirements
FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Specialized agribusiness banking with equipment loans, operating loans, farm financial consulting, and FSA guarantees
Comprehensive digital banking including online bill pay, mobile deposit, mobile wallet, and card guard fraud protection
24/7 ATM and debit card support via dedicated phone line
Beginning farmer program and farm financial consulting for agricultural clients
Strong community engagement with documented charitable donations and local support
cons: Geographic service area appears limited to Indiana based on available location information
No mention of interest rates, APRs, or loan terms on public website
Limited information about account minimums, overdraft fees, or other banking costs
No apparent 24/7 customer service line (business/agricultural banking: 260-589-2426 and 888-589-1003)
Website does not provide details on credit card rewards, mortgage rate products, or competitive advantages
best_for: Farmers and agricultural operations seeking specialized agribusiness banking services and farm financial consulting
Small business owners prioritizing personal banker relationships over national bank automation
Indiana residents valuing community bank support and local decision-making over large corporate institutions
diagnosis: First Bank of Berne is best for Indiana-based farmers, agricultural businesses, and small business owners who prioritize personal relationships with their banker and community values over technological innovation or national branch access. The main caveat is limited geographic availability and lack of transparent pricing information on their public website—prospective customers should contact them directly to verify service availability and compare rates and fees.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
